  # TODO: use real signals (like activated/settings changed, etc.) to speed up re-enabling buttons
  self._tethering_toggle_btn.set_enabled(True)
  self._network_metered_btn.set_enabled(lambda: not tethering_active and bool(self._wifi_manager.ipv4_address))
  self._tethering_toggle_btn.set_checked(tethering_active)

  # Update wi-fi button with ssid and ip address
  # TODO: make sure we handle hidden ssids
  connected_network = next((network for network in networks if network.is_connected), None)
  self._wifi_button.set_text(normalize_ssid(connected_network.ssid) if connected_network is not None else "wi-fi")
  self._wifi_button.set_value(self._wifi_manager.ipv4_address or "not connected")
  if connected_network is not None:
    strength = WifiIcon.get_strength_icon_idx(connected_network.strength)
    if strength == 2:
      strength_icon = self._wifi_full_txt
    elif strength == 1:
      strength_icon = self._wifi_medium_txt
    else:
      strength_icon = self._wifi_low_txt
    self._wifi_button.set_icon(strength_icon)
  else:
    self._wifi_button.set_icon(self._wifi_slash_txt)

  # Update network metered
  self._network_metered_btn.set_value(
    {
      MeteredType.UNKNOWN: 'default',
      MeteredType.YES: 'metered',
      MeteredType.NO: 'unmetered'
    }.get(self._wifi_manager.current_network_metered, 'default'))

  # TODO: use real signals (like activated/settings changed, etc.) to speed up re-enabling buttons
  self._tethering_toggle_btn.set_enabled(True)
  self._network_metered_btn.set_enabled(lambda: not tethering_active and bool(self._wifi_manager.ipv4_address))
  self._tethering_toggle_btn.set_checked(tethering_active)

  # Update wi-fi button with ssid and ip address
  # TODO: make sure we handle hidden ssids
  connecting_ssid = self._wifi_manager.connecting_to_ssid
  connected_network = next((network for network in networks if network.is_connected), None)
  if connecting_ssid:

    self._wifi_button.set_text(normalize_ssid(connecting_ssid))

    strength = WifiIcon.get_strength_icon_idx(display_network.strength)

  else:
    self._wifi_button.set_icon(self._wifi_slash_txt)

    }.get(self._wifi_manager.current_network_metered, 'default'))

def find_differences(video1, video2):
  with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as tmpdir:
    tmpdir = Path(tmpdir)

    print(f"Extracting frames from {video1}...")
    frames1_dir = tmpdir / "frames1"
    frames1_dir.mkdir()
    frames1 = extract_frames(video1, frames1_dir)

    print(f"Extracting frames from {video2}...")
    frames2_dir = tmpdir / "frames2"
    frames2_dir.mkdir()
    frames2 = extract_frames(video2, frames2_dir)

    if len(frames1) != len(frames2):
      print(f"WARNING: Frame count mismatch: {len(frames1)} vs {len(frames2)}")
      min_frames = min(len(frames1), len(frames2))
      frames1 = frames1[:min_frames]
      frames2 = frames2[:min_frames]

    print(f"Comparing {len(frames1)} frames...")
    different_frames = []
    frame_data = []

    for i, (f1, f2) in enumerate(zip(frames1, frames2, strict=False)):
      is_different = not compare_frames(f1, f2)
      if is_different:
        different_frames.append(i)

      if i < 10 or i >= len(frames1) - 10 or is_different:
        frame_data.append({'index': i, 'different': is_different, 'frame1_url': frame_to_data_url(f1), 'frame2_url': frame_to_data_url(f2)})

    return different_frames, frame_data, len(frames1)

def runEmbedMode():
    """
    CLI handler for 'guider embed' command.

    Generates embedding vector(s) for the given text using the
    H-Chat (AzureOpenAI-compatible) Embeddings API.

    Command format:
        guider embed [TEXT] -q KEY:VALUE[,KEY:VALUE...]

    -q options (all optional):
        EMBEDTEXT:string      - Text to embed (alternative to positional arg)
        EMBEDFILE:path        - Read text from file (plain text or JSON)
        EMBEDMODEL:model-id   - Embedding model (default: text-embedding-3-small)
        EMBEDDIM:256          - Reduce output dimensions (text-embedding-3+ only)
        EMBEDOUTPUT:path      - Save result JSON to file
        EMBEDBATCH:1          - Treat each line of file as a separate input

    Environment variables (fallback):
        CUSTOM_API_KEY / EMBED_API_KEY      - API key
        CUSTOM_BASE_URL / EMBED_BASE_URL    - API base URL
        EMBED_MODEL                         - Default embedding model
        EMBED_PROJECT_ID                    - Project ID for project API keys

    Examples:
        guider embed "Artificial intelligence mimics human learning."
        guider embed -q EMBEDFILE:docs.txt,EMBEDOUTPUT:vecs.json
        guider embed "sample text" -q EMBEDMODEL:text-embedding-3-large,EMBEDDIM:256
    """
    try:
        # Get text: positional arg > EMBEDTEXT > EMBEDFILE
        text = None
        if SysMgr.hasMainArg():
            text = SysMgr.getMainArg()
        elif len(sys.argv) > 2 and not sys.argv[2].startswith("-"):
            text = sys.argv[2]

        embedText = LLMMgr._getEnvironValue("EMBEDTEXT")
        embedFile = LLMMgr._getEnvironValue("EMBEDFILE")

        if text:
            inputText = text
        elif embedText:
            inputText = embedText
        elif embedFile:
            with open(embedFile, "r", encoding="utf-8") as f:
                raw = f.read()
            batch = LLMMgr._parseBoolValue(
                LLMMgr._getEnvironValue("EMBEDBATCH")
            )
            if batch:
                inputText = [
                    line for line in raw.splitlines() if line.strip()
                ]
            else:
                inputText = raw.strip()
        else:
            SysMgr.printErr("no input text provided")
            SysMgr.printWarn(
                "Usage:\n"
                "  guider embed [TEXT]\n"
                "  guider embed -q EMBEDFILE:path\n"
                "  guider embed -q EMBEDTEXT:string\n\n"
                "Examples:\n"
                "  guider embed 'Artificial intelligence mimics human learning.'\n"
                "  guider embed -q EMBEDFILE:docs.txt,EMBEDOUTPUT:vecs.json\n"
                "  guider embed 'sample text' -q EMBEDMODEL:text-embedding-3-large,EMBEDDIM:256"
            )
            sys.exit(1)

        # Options
        model = LLMMgr._getEnvironValue("EMBEDMODEL") or os.getenv(
            "EMBED_MODEL"
        )
        dimOpt = LLMMgr._getEnvironValue("EMBEDDIM")
        dimensions = int(dimOpt) if dimOpt else None
        outputPath = LLMMgr._getEnvironValue("EMBEDOUTPUT")

        # Create embedder
        embedder = LLMMgr.CustomOpenAIEmbedder(model=model)

        SysMgr.printInfo(
            "embedding with model: {0}{1}".format(
                embedder.model,
                " (dim={0})".format(dimensions) if dimensions else "",
            )
        )

        resp = embedder.embed(inputText, dimensions=dimensions)

        count = len(resp.embeddings)
        dim = len(resp.embeddings[0]) if resp.embeddings else 0
        SysMgr.printInfo(
            "generated {0} embedding(s), {1} dimensions".format(count, dim)
        )
        if resp.usage:
            SysMgr.printInfo(
                "tokens used: {0}".format(
                    UtilMgr.convNum(resp.usage.get("total_tokens", "n/a"))
                )
            )

        json_mod = SysMgr.getPkg("json")

        result = {
            "model": resp.model,
            "count": count,
            "dimensions": dim,
            "usage": resp.usage,
            "embeddings": resp.embeddings,
        }

        if outputPath:
            with open(outputPath, "w", encoding="utf-8") as f:
                json_mod.dump(result, f)
            SysMgr.printInfo("saved to: {0}".format(outputPath))
        else:
            # Print first vector preview to stdout (truncated)
            if resp.embeddings:
                preview = resp.embeddings[0][:8]
                SysMgr.printPipe(
                    "embedding[0] (first 8 values): {0} ...".format(
                        preview
                    )
                )

        sys.exit(0)

    except Exception as e:
        SysMgr.printErr("embed command failed: {0}".format(str(e)))
        import traceback

        traceback.print_exc()
        sys.exit(1)

Usage:

Description:

      3. Find TopK most similar chunks by cosine similarity
      4. Send retrieved context + query to LLM for final answer

Options:

Workflow:

        # export LLM_PROVIDER=custom-claude
        # {0:1} {1:1} "Analyze boot time" -q RAGDOCS:boot.log

          [ a:all | c:cpu | C:compress | e:encode
            g:general | s:signal ]
    -s  <DIR|FILE>                           save trace data
    -f                                       force execution
    -u                                       run in the background
    -W  <SEC>                                wait before starting
    -b  <SIZE:KB>                            set buffer size
    -D                                       trace task dependency
    -t  <SYSCALL>                            trace syscall
    -B  <DIR|FILE>                           set command script path
    -w  <TIME:FILE{:VALUE}>                  set additional command
    -U  <NAME:FUNC|ADDR:FILE|COMM|PID:RET>   set user event
    -K  <NAME:FUNC|ADDR:ARGS>                set kernel event
    -R  <INTERVAL:TIME:TERM>                 set repeat count
    -L  <PATH>                               set log file

  [report]
    -a                                       show all stats and events
    -o  <DIR|FILE>                           set output path
    -S  <CHARACTER>                          sort by key
          [ c:cpu / m:memory / p:pid / b:block
            w:wfc / n:new / r:runtime / f:file ]
    -P                                       group threads by process
    -p  <TID>                                show preemption info
    -O  <CORE>                               set core filter
    -L  <RES:PER>                            set graph layout (TOTAL PER: 6)
    -m  <ROWS:COLS:SYSTEM>                   set terminal size
    -i  <SEC>                                set interval
    -Q                                       print all rows in a stream
    -q  <NAME{:VALUE}>                       set environment variables

  [common]

    -C  <PATH>                               set config path
    -A  <ARCH>                               set CPU type
    -c  <EVENT:COND>                         set trace event
    -E  <DIR>                                set cache dir path

    -z  <COMM|TID:MASK{:CONT}>               set CPU affinity
    -Y  <VALUES>                             set sched

            CLASS:{WHICH:}PRIO:TID|COMM ]
    -v                                       verbose

Reference:
    - Viewing the profiled data
        https://ui.perfetto.dev
                    """
                    )

                    helpStr += r"""
  * uprobe
	p[:[GRP/]EVENT] PATH:OFFSET [FETCHARGS] : Set a uprobe
	r[:[GRP/]EVENT] PATH:OFFSET [FETCHARGS] : Set a return uprobe (uretprobe)
	p[:[GRP/]EVENT] PATH:OFFSET%return [FETCHARGS] : Set a return uprobe (uretprobe)
	-:[GRP/]EVENT                           : Clear uprobe or uretprobe event

	GRP           : Group name. If omitted, "uprobes" is the default value.
	EVENT         : Event name. If omitted, the event name is generated based on PATH+OFFSET.
	PATH          : Path to an executable or a library.
	OFFSET        : Offset where the probe is inserted.
	OFFSET%return : Offset where the return probe is inserted.
	FETCHARGS     : Arguments. Each probe can have up to 128 args.
	 %REG         : Fetch register REG
	 @ADDR        : Fetch memory at ADDR (ADDR should be in userspace)
	 @+OFFSET     : Fetch memory at OFFSET (OFFSET from same file as PATH)
	 $stackN      : Fetch Nth entry of stack (N >= 0)
	 $stack       : Fetch stack address.
	 $retval      : Fetch return value.(\*1)
	 $comm        : Fetch current task comm.
	 +|-[u]OFFS(FETCHARG) : Fetch memory at FETCHARG +|- OFFS address.(\*2)(\*3)
	 \IMM         : Store an immediate value to the argument.
	 NAME=FETCHARG     : Set NAME as the argument name of FETCHARG.
	 FETCHARG:TYPE     : Set TYPE as the type of FETCHARG. Currently, basic types
						 (u8/u16/u32/u64/s8/s16/s32/s64), hexadecimal types
						 (x8/x16/x32/x64), "string" and bitfield are supported.
	(\*1) only for return probe.
	(\*2) this is useful for fetching a field of data structures.
	(\*3) Unlike kprobe event, "u" prefix will just be ignored, becuse uprobe
		  events can access only user-space memory.

  * kprobe
     p[:[GRP/][EVENT]] [MOD:]SYM[+offs]|MEMADDR [FETCHARGS]        : Set a probe
     r[MAXACTIVE][:[GRP/][EVENT]] [MOD:]SYM[+0] [FETCHARGS]        : Set a return probe
     p[:[GRP/][EVENT]] [MOD:]SYM[+0]%return [FETCHARGS]    : Set a return probe
     -:[GRP/][EVENT]                                               : Clear a probe

    GRP            : Group name. If omitted, use "kprobes" for it.
    EVENT          : Event name. If omitted, the event name is generated
                     based on SYM+offs or MEMADDR.
    MOD            : Module name which has given SYM.
    SYM[+offs]     : Symbol+offset where the probe is inserted.
    SYM%return     : Return address of the symbol
    MEMADDR        : Address where the probe is inserted.
    MAXACTIVE      : Maximum number of instances of the specified function that
                     can be probed simultaneously, or 0 for the default value
                     as defined in Documentation/trace/kprobes.rst section 1.3.1.
    FETCHARGS      : Arguments. Each probe can have up to 128 args.
     %REG          : Fetch register REG
     @ADDR         : Fetch memory at ADDR (ADDR should be in kernel)
     @SYM[+|-offs] : Fetch memory at SYM +|- offs (SYM should be a data symbol)
     $stackN       : Fetch Nth entry of stack (N >= 0)
     $stack        : Fetch stack address.
     $argN         : Fetch the Nth function argument. (N >= 1) (\*1)
     $retval       : Fetch return value.(\*2)
     $comm         : Fetch current task comm.
     +|-[u]OFFS(FETCHARG) : Fetch memory at FETCHARG +|- OFFS address.(\*3)(\*4)
     \IMM          : Store an immediate value to the argument.
     NAME=FETCHARG : Set NAME as the argument name of FETCHARG.
     FETCHARG:TYPE : Set TYPE as the type of FETCHARG. Currently, basic types
                     (u8/u16/u32/u64/s8/s16/s32/s64), hexadecimal types
                     (x8/x16/x32/x64), "string", "ustring" and bitfield
                     are supported.
     (\*1) only for the probe on function entry (offs == 0).
     (\*2) only for return probe.
     (\*3) this is useful for fetching a field of data structures.
     (\*4) "u" means user-space dereference. See :ref:`user_mem_access`.
                    """

                cmd = os.environ["CMDLINE"]

    -L  <RES:PER>               set graph layout (TOTAL PER: 6)
          [ C:CPU | D:Delay | M:Memory | I:IO ]
    -F  [svg/png/pdf/ps/eps]    set image format
    -E  <DIR>                   set cache dir path
    -C  <PATH>                  set config file
    -O  <CORE>                  set core filter
    -q  <NAME{:VALUE}>          set environment variables
    -v                          verbose

Segments:
    Long Color   : Run
    Upper Black  : Wait
    Upper Red    : Preempted
    Lower Purple : Read
    Lower Cyan   : Write
    Horizon Gray : Off

Troubleshooting:
    - "No module named pkg_resources" when installing svgwrite package using pip
        # curl https://bootstrap.pypa.io/ez_setup.py | python3

Commands(%s):
    acc       Print accumulation stats for specific values [NAME:VAR|REG|VAL]

    exec      Execute the external command [CMD]

    fadvise   Give advise about access of file [ADDR:LENGTH/ENDADDR:ADVISE]
    filter    Print only filtered context [VAR|ADDR|REG|RETTIME|RETVAL:OP(EQ/DF/INC/BT/LT):VAR|VAL:SIZE]

    getenv    Print specific environment variable [VAR]
    getret    Print return value [CMD]
    help      Print help
    hidden    Hide the function call

    -g  <COMM|TID{:FILE}>                    set task filter
    -C  <PATH>                               set config path
    -A  <ARCH>                               set CPU type
    -c  <EVENT:COND>                         set trace event
    -E  <DIR>                                set cache dir path
    -k  <COMM|TID:SIG{:CONT}>                set signal
    -z  <COMM|TID:MASK{:CONT}>               set CPU affinity
    -Y  <VALUES>                             set sched
          [ POLICY:PRIO|TIME{:TID|COMM:CONT}
            CLASS:{WHICH:}PRIO:TID|COMM ]
    -v                                       verbose

Reference:
    - Viewing the profiled data
        https://ui.perfetto.dev

                    )

                    helpStr += r"""
  * uprobe
	p[:[GRP/]EVENT] PATH:OFFSET [FETCHARGS] : Set a uprobe
	r[:[GRP/]EVENT] PATH:OFFSET [FETCHARGS] : Set a return uprobe (uretprobe)
	p[:[GRP/]EVENT] PATH:OFFSET%return [FETCHARGS] : Set a return uprobe (uretprobe)
	-:[GRP/]EVENT                           : Clear uprobe or uretprobe event

	GRP           : Group name. If omitted, "uprobes" is the default value.
	EVENT         : Event name. If omitted, the event name is generated based on PATH+OFFSET.
	PATH          : Path to an executable or a library.
	OFFSET        : Offset where the probe is inserted.
	OFFSET%return : Offset where the return probe is inserted.
	FETCHARGS     : Arguments. Each probe can have up to 128 args.
	 %REG         : Fetch register REG
	 @ADDR        : Fetch memory at ADDR (ADDR should be in userspace)
	 @+OFFSET     : Fetch memory at OFFSET (OFFSET from same file as PATH)
	 $stackN      : Fetch Nth entry of stack (N >= 0)
	 $stack       : Fetch stack address.
	 $retval      : Fetch return value.(\*1)
	 $comm        : Fetch current task comm.
	 +|-[u]OFFS(FETCHARG) : Fetch memory at FETCHARG +|- OFFS address.(\*2)(\*3)
	 \IMM         : Store an immediate value to the argument.
	 NAME=FETCHARG     : Set NAME as the argument name of FETCHARG.
	 FETCHARG:TYPE     : Set TYPE as the type of FETCHARG. Currently, basic types
						 (u8/u16/u32/u64/s8/s16/s32/s64), hexadecimal types
						 (x8/x16/x32/x64), "string" and bitfield are supported.
	(\*1) only for return probe.
	(\*2) this is useful for fetching a field of data structures.
	(\*3) Unlike kprobe event, "u" prefix will just be ignored, becuse uprobe
		  events can access only user-space memory.

  * kprobe
     p[:[GRP/][EVENT]] [MOD:]SYM[+offs]|MEMADDR [FETCHARGS]        : Set a probe
     r[MAXACTIVE][:[GRP/][EVENT]] [MOD:]SYM[+0] [FETCHARGS]        : Set a return probe
     p[:[GRP/][EVENT]] [MOD:]SYM[+0]%return [FETCHARGS]    : Set a return probe
     -:[GRP/][EVENT]                                               : Clear a probe

    GRP            : Group name. If omitted, use "kprobes" for it.
    EVENT          : Event name. If omitted, the event name is generated
                     based on SYM+offs or MEMADDR.
    MOD            : Module name which has given SYM.
    SYM[+offs]     : Symbol+offset where the probe is inserted.
    SYM%return     : Return address of the symbol
    MEMADDR        : Address where the probe is inserted.
    MAXACTIVE      : Maximum number of instances of the specified function that
                     can be probed simultaneously, or 0 for the default value
                     as defined in Documentation/trace/kprobes.rst section 1.3.1.
    FETCHARGS      : Arguments. Each probe can have up to 128 args.
     %REG          : Fetch register REG
     @ADDR         : Fetch memory at ADDR (ADDR should be in kernel)
     @SYM[+|-offs] : Fetch memory at SYM +|- offs (SYM should be a data symbol)
     $stackN       : Fetch Nth entry of stack (N >= 0)
     $stack        : Fetch stack address.
     $argN         : Fetch the Nth function argument. (N >= 1) (\*1)
     $retval       : Fetch return value.(\*2)
     $comm         : Fetch current task comm.
     +|-[u]OFFS(FETCHARG) : Fetch memory at FETCHARG +|- OFFS address.(\*3)(\*4)
     \IMM          : Store an immediate value to the argument.
     NAME=FETCHARG : Set NAME as the argument name of FETCHARG.
     FETCHARG:TYPE : Set TYPE as the type of FETCHARG. Currently, basic types
                     (u8/u16/u32/u64/s8/s16/s32/s64), hexadecimal types
                     (x8/x16/x32/x64), "string", "ustring" and bitfield
                     are supported.
     (\*1) only for the probe on function entry (offs == 0).
     (\*2) only for return probe.
     (\*3) this is useful for fetching a field of data structures.
     (\*4) "u" means user-space dereference. See :ref:`user_mem_access`.
                    """

def reqHandler(signum, frame):
    # define error message sender function #
    def _sendErrMsg(connObj, msg):
        connObj.write('{"error": %s}' % msg)

    def _sendInfoMsg(connObj, msg):
        connObj.write('{"info": %s}' % msg)

    def _printErr(msg, reason=False):
        SysMgr.printErr(msg, reason=reason)
        _sendErrMsg(connObj, msg)

    def _printInfo(msg):
        SysMgr.printInfo(msg)
        _sendInfoMsg(connObj, msg)

    # define command handler #
    def _handleCmd(connObj, cmd, cliProc):
        # update event status #
        SysMgr.updateEventStatus()

        # set final command #
        cmdList = cmd.split("|")
        mainCmd = cmdList[0].lower()
        cmdOpt = cmdList[1:] if len(cmdList) > 1 else []

        # handle watch request #
        if mainCmd == "watch":
            SysMgr.printInfo("registered %s for event watch" % cliProc)
            SysMgr.eventSubList.append([cliProc, connObj])
            return
        # handle limit request #
        elif SysMgr.isLimitMode(mainCmd):
            # save target flags #
            origProcessEnable = SysMgr.processEnable
            origTargetExe = SysMgr.environList.pop("TARGETEXE", None)
            origTargetCmd = SysMgr.environList.pop("TARGETCMD", None)

            # set target flags #
            upperOpts = list(map(str.upper, cmdOpt))
            SysMgr.processEnable = "PROCESS" in upperOpts
            for name in ("TARGETEXE", "TARGETCMD"):
                if name in upperOpts:
                    SysMgr.environList[name] = ["SET"]

            # apply limit #
            try:
                ret = SysMgr.applyLimitVars(
                    {mainCmd.upper(): [":".join(cmdOpt[:1])]}
                )
                if ret:
                    _printInfo(
                        "applied %s command for %s"
                        % (
                            cmd,
                            SysMgr.getCommList(
                                ret,
                                cmdline="TARGETCMD" in SysMgr.environList,
                            ),
                        )
                    )
                else:
                    raise Exception("no task")
            except:
                _printErr("failed to execute %s command" % cmd)

            # close connection #
            try:
                connObj.close()
            except:
                pass
            finally:
                # recover process flag #
                SysMgr.processEnable = origProcessEnable
                for flag, name in (
                    (origTargetExe, "TARGETEXE"),
                    (origTargetCmd, "TARGETCMD"),
                ):
                    if not flag:
                        SysMgr.environList.pop(name, None)
                return

        # check the maximum number of jobs #
        nrMaxJob = UtilMgr.getEnvironNum("NRMAXJOB", False, 0, False)
        if nrMaxJob:
            nrJobs = len(SysMgr.getEventTaskList(json=True))
            if nrMaxJob <= nrJobs:
                _printErr(
                    (
                        "failed to run a new job "
                        "because %s jobs are already running (MAX:%s)"
                    )
                    % (UtilMgr.convNum(nrJobs), UtilMgr.convNum(nrMaxJob))
                )
                return

        # backup items before worker task creation #
        localServObj = SysMgr.localServObj
        eventCommandList = SysMgr.eventCommandList

        # check save mode #
        isSaveMode = mainCmd in ("save", "saveraw", "savemin")

        # create a new worker process and parent return #
        pid = SysMgr.createProcess()
        if pid > 0:
            # close connection to decrease refcnt of socket in parent #
            connObj.close()

            # register event task info #
            SysMgr.addEventInfo(cmd, pid)

            # increase report number #
            if isSaveMode or mainCmd in ("cpuprof", "memprof"):
                SysMgr.nrReport += 1

            return

        # apply new options #
        sys.argv = sys.argv[:2]
        optStr = " ".join(cmdOpt)
        if optStr:
            try:
                SysMgr.updateEnvironVars(optStr)
                SysMgr.parseAnalOption(optStr)
            except:
                _printErr("failed to apply options '%s'" % optStr, True)
                return

        # set default handling environment #
        SysMgr.setSignal(SysMgr.commSig, signal.SIG_IGN)
        SysMgr.setSignal(signal.SIGINT, SysMgr.termHandler)
        SysMgr.clearExitFunc()
        SysMgr.encodeEnable = False
        SysMgr.colorEnable = False
        SysMgr.printEnable = True
        SysMgr.streamEnable = True
        SysMgr.eventCommandList = eventCommandList
        SysMgr.progressEnable = False
        origOutPath = SysMgr.outPath
        if origOutPath:
            origOutPath = SysMgr.convFullPath(origOutPath)
        SysMgr.outPath = None
        if not SysMgr.getOption("i"):
            SysMgr.intervalEnable = 1

        # mark SERVERTASK #
        SysMgr.addEnvironVar("SERVERTASK")

        # define a socket monitor function #
        def _monitorConn(connObj):
            # wait for disconnection #
            while 1:
                try:
                    # wait for socket event #
                    selectObj = SysMgr.getPkg("select")
                    selectObj.select([connObj.socket], [], [])

                    # check socket event #
                    if NetworkMgr.check(connObj.socket):
                        time.sleep(0.1)
                        continue

                    SysMgr.printInfo(
                        "disconnected from %s for '%s' command"
                        % (cliProc, cmd)
                    )
                    os.kill(os.getpid(), signal.SIGINT)
                    return
                except SystemExit:
                    sys.exit(0)
                except:
                    SysMgr.printWarn(
                        "failed to monitor the socket for %s of %s"
                        % (cmd, cliProc),
                        True,
                        True,
                    )

        # create a socket monitor task for handling disconnection #
        threadObj = SysMgr.getPkg("threading")
        tobj = threadObj.Thread(target=_monitorConn, args=[connObj])
        tobj.daemon = True
        tobj.start()

        # define notify function #
        def _printExecutedCmd():
            SysMgr.printInfo(
                "executed '%s' command from %s" % (cmd, cliProc)
            )

        # check command #
        isDrawMode = mainCmd in SysMgr.getCmdList().get("visual", {})
        if not (
            mainCmd
            in (
                "bdtop",
                "bdmon",
                "control",
                "cpuprof",
                "event",
                "dir",
                "dirlist",
                "download",
                "dump",
                "getconf",
                "getpeak",
                "getpkg",
                "jobs",
                "kill",
                "logcat",
                "logmon",
                "memmon",
                "memprof",
                "memtop",
                "pkglist",
                "ps",
                "pslist",
                "remove",
                "resmon",
                "ressum",
                "restop",
                "restopsum",
                "save",
                "saveraw",
                "savemin",
                "sysdump",
                "sysinfo",
                "syslist",
                "taskresmon",
                "tkill",
            )
            or isDrawMode
        ):
            errMsg = "no support '%s' command" % mainCmd
            _printErr(errMsg)
            sys.exit(-1)

        # print status #
        _printExecutedCmd()

        # set task name #
        SysMgr.setComm("guider_" + mainCmd)

        # clear monitoring buffer #
        if (
            mainCmd
            not in (
                "dump",
                "sysdump",
            )
            and not isSaveMode
        ):
            SysMgr.clearProcBuffer()

        # -------------------- execute command -------------------- #
        if mainCmd in ("dump", "sysdump"):
            # summarize monitoring buffer #
            SysMgr.printFd = MemoryFile(name="buf")
            TaskAnalyzer.printIntervalUsage(
                onlySummary=True, onlyTotal=(mainCmd == "sysdump")
            )

            # send summary stats in JSON #
            SysMgr.jsonEnable = True
            SysMgr.printFd.seek(0)
            stats, memstat = TaskAnalyzer.getStatsFile(
                "tmp", handle=SysMgr.printFd
            )
            SysMgr.printFd = connObj
            connObj.write(UtilMgr.convDict2Str(stats, pretty=False))

        elif isSaveMode:
            # update output path #
            SysMgr.outPath = origOutPath

            # build command string #
            cmdstr = mainCmd.upper()
            if cmdOpt:
                cmdstr += ":" + "|".join(cmdOpt)

            # WARN: not support timer in this context #

            # define file path sender #
            def _pathSender(connObj, cmd):
                # get real path #
                path = SysMgr.lastReportPath
                if path:
                    name, ext = path.rsplit(".", 1)
                    name += "_%s" % SysMgr.pid
                    path = name + "." + ext

                # send result #
                pathDict = {
                    "cmd": cmd,
                    "path": path,
                    "size": UtilMgr.getFileSize(path, False),
                }
                connObj.write(UtilMgr.convDict2Str(pathDict, pretty=False))

            # register return function #
            SysMgr.addExitFunc(_pathSender, [connObj, cmd])

            # handle save command #
            try:
                SysMgr.topInstance.handleEventCmd(
                    cmdstr, "CUSTOM", False, False
                )
            except SystemExit:
                pass
            except:
                _printErr("failed to handle %s" % mainCmd, True)

        elif mainCmd in ("resmon", "taskresmon", "restop"):
            # start sending stats in JSON #
            SysMgr.jsonEnable = mainCmd in ("resmon", "taskresmon")
            SysMgr.printFd = connObj

            # add environ var only for task monitoring #
            if mainCmd == "taskresmon":
                SysMgr.addEnvironVar("TASKSTREAM")
                SysMgr.taskStreamEnable = True

            # return and continue #
            if SysMgr.isTopMode():
                return
            else:
                main("top")
                sys.exit(0)

        elif mainCmd in ("ressum", "restopsum"):
            if not cmdOpt:
                _printErr("no target file path")
                sys.exit(-1)
            for f in cmdOpt:
                if not os.path.exists(f):
                    _printErr("no '%s' file" % f)
                    sys.exit(-1)

            # start sending stats in JSON #
            SysMgr.jsonEnable = mainCmd in ("ressum")
            SysMgr.printFd = connObj

            # summarize report file #
            TaskAnalyzer.doSumReport(cmdOpt)

        elif mainCmd in ("getconf"):

            SysMgr.jsonEnable = True

            SysMgr.jsonEnable = True

            # get peak items #
            peakList = SysMgr.getPeakList()

        elif mainCmd in ("bdmon", "bdtop"):
            # start sending stats in JSON #

            SysMgr.addEnvironVar("FLUSH")

            targets = [
                "binder/binder_transaction",
                "binder/binder_transaction_received",
            ]
            cb = LogMgr.binderHandler
            parser = LogMgr.parseBinderData
            LogMgr.printTrace(False, targets=targets, cb=cb, parser=parser)

        elif mainCmd in ("remove", "download"):
            if not cmdOpt:
                _printErr("no target file path")
                sys.exit(-1)

            # define getter #
            def _getPacket(fd):
                while 1:
                    try:
                        packet = fd.read(connObj.sendSize)
                        if packet == b"":
                            raise Exception()
                        yield packet
                    except GeneratorExit:
                        return
                    except SystemExit:
                        sys.exit(0)
                    except:
                        yield None

            def _remove(d):
                flist = UtilMgr.getFileList(
                    [d], sort=True, exceptDir=False
                )
                res = SysMgr.removeFiles(flist)
                if res:
                    _printInfo("removed '%s'" % ",".join(res))
                else:
                    _printErr("no removed file")

            def _download(d):
                try:
                    fd = open(d, "rb")

                    sys.exit(0)
                except:
                    _printErr("failed to open '%s'" % d, True)
                    sys.exit(-1)

                # start sending file data #
                for packet in _getPacket(fd):
                    if not packet:
                        break
                    connObj.write(packet)

            # handle files #
            for d in cmdOpt:
                # validate path #
                if "NOLIMITPATH" in SysMgr.environList:
                    pass
                # no out path #
                elif not origOutPath:
                    _printErr("no output path in Guider")
                    sys.exit(-1)
                # absolute path #
                elif d.startswith("/"):
                    if not d.startswith(origOutPath):
                        _printErr("'%s' is not in '%s'" % (d, origOutPath))
                        sys.exit(-1)
                # relative path #
                elif not os.path.exists(os.path.join(origOutPath, d)):
                    _printErr("'%s' is not in '%s'" % (d, origOutPath))
                    sys.exit(-1)

                # remove #
                if mainCmd == "remove":
                    _remove(d)
                # download #
                elif mainCmd == "download":

                if not origOutPath:

                if not os.path.exists(origOutPath):
                    cmdOpt = [os.path.dirname(origOutPath)]
                elif os.path.isdir(origOutPath):
                    cmdOpt = [origOutPath]
                else:
                    cmdOpt = [os.path.dirname(origOutPath)]

            # start sending stats in JSON #
            SysMgr.jsonEnable = mainCmd == "dirlist"
            SysMgr.printFd = connObj
            SysMgr.showAll = True

            # check dir #
            cmdOpt = UtilMgr.cleanItem(cmdOpt)
            if not cmdOpt:
                _printErr("failed to get directory info")
                sys.exit(-1)

            # list directories #
            for d in cmdOpt:
                SysMgr.printDirs(d)

        elif mainCmd in ("getpkg", "pkglist"):

            SysMgr.jsonEnable = mainCmd == "pkglist"
            SysMgr.printFd = connObj
            SysMgr.showAll = True

            # get package list #
            SysMgr.addEnvironVar("GETPKGLIST")
            SysMgr.doPerfetto()

        elif mainCmd in ("sysinfo", "syslist"):
            # start sending stats in JSON #
            SysMgr.jsonEnable = mainCmd == "syslist"
            SysMgr.printFd = connObj

            # print system info #
            SysMgr.doPrintInfo()

        elif mainCmd in ("ps", "pslist"):
            # start sending stats in JSON #
            SysMgr.jsonEnable = mainCmd == "pslist"
            SysMgr.printFd = connObj

            # print process list #
            SysMgr.doPs()

            # start sending stats in JSON #

            SysMgr.printFd = connObj

            # start monitoring memory #
            SysMgr.addEnvironVar("MEMTOP")

        elif mainCmd in ("logcat", "logmon"):
            # start sending stats in JSON #
            SysMgr.jsonEnable = mainCmd == "logmon"
            SysMgr.printFd = connObj

            # start printing android logs #
            LogMgr.printAndLog(True)

        elif mainCmd == "jobs":
            # set JSON attribute #
            SysMgr.jsonEnable = True

            # send event task list #
            connObj.write(
                UtilMgr.convDict2Str(
                    SysMgr.getEventTaskList(), pretty=False
                )
            )

        elif mainCmd in ("kill", "tkill"):
            # set JSON attribute #
            SysMgr.jsonEnable = True

            isThread = mainCmd == "tkill"

            # convert target list #
            try:
                argList = UtilMgr.cleanItem(("".join(cmdOpt)).split(","))
            except SystemExit:
                sys.exit(0)
            except:
                pass

            # convert items to pid #
            targets = SysMgr.convTaskList(argList, isThread=isThread)
            if targets:
                targetList = targets
            else:
                targetList = argList

            # send signal #
            ret = SysMgr.sendSignalProcs(
                signal.SIGKILL, targetList, isThread=isThread
            )

            # send event task list #
            connObj.write(UtilMgr.convDict2Str(ret, pretty=False))

        elif mainCmd in ("control", "event"):
            if not cmdOpt:
                _printErr("no %s value" % mainCmd)
                sys.exit(-1)

            # create a UDP socket #
            cliObj = NetworkMgr(
                "client", localServObj.ip, localServObj.port, tcp=False
            )

            # send commands #
            for ccmd in cmdOpt:
                # make command #
                ccmd = ccmd.upper()
                if ccmd.startswith("EVENT_CMD_"):
                    pass
                elif ccmd.startswith("CMD_"):
                    ccmd = "EVENT_" + ccmd
                else:
                    ccmd = "EVENT_CMD_" + ccmd

                if mainCmd == "event":
                    ccmd = ccmd.replace("_CMD", "")

                # send command #
                cliObj.send(ccmd)

                _printInfo(
                    "sent '%s' command to server(%s:%s)"
                    % (ccmd, cliObj.ip, cliObj.port)
                )

        elif mainCmd in ("cpuprof", "memprof") or isDrawMode:
            # get select object #
            selectObj = SysMgr.getPkg("select")

            # add environment var to merge env list #
            SysMgr.addEnvironVar("MERGEENVLIST")

            # set command set #
            if isDrawMode:
                gcmd = mainCmd
            elif mainCmd == "cpuprof":
                gcmd = "sperf"
            elif mainCmd == "memprof":
                gcmd = "hprof"
            else:
                _printErr(
                    "failed to recognize '%s' command from %s"
                    % (mainCmd, cliProc)
                )
                sys.exit(-1)

            cmdSet = [gcmd] + cmdOpt + ["-J"]

            # cleanup directories #
            SysMgr.cleanupDirs()

            # set report path #
            SysMgr.outPath = origOutPath
            SysMgr.setReportPath(origOutPath, mainCmd)

            # launch Guider command #
            try:
                ret = SysMgr.launchGuider(
                    cmdSet,
                    pipe=True,
                    stderr=True,
                    stream=True,
                    logo=False,
                    log=False,
                    keepOut=True,
                )
                if not ret:
                    sys.exit(-1)
            except SystemExit:
                sys.exit(0)
            except:
                _printErr(
                    "failed to launch %s(%s)"
                    % (__module__, " ".join(cmdSet)),
                    reason=True,
                )
                sys.exit(-1)

            # get pid and pipe #
            pid, pipe = ret
            pipeObj = os.fdopen(pipe.fileno(), "r")
            SysMgr.setBlock(pipe.fileno(), False)

            # remove socket timeout #
            connObj.timeout(None)

            try:
                while 1:
                    [rdList, _, _] = selectObj.select(
                        [pipeObj, connObj.socket], [], [], 1
                    )
                    for rd in rdList:
                        # final output from launcher #
                        if rd == pipeObj:
                            res = pipeObj.read()
                            if res:
                                connObj.write(res)
                            connObj.close()
                            raise ValueError()
                        # signal from client #
                        elif rd == connObj.socket:
                            # check socket event #
                            if not NetworkMgr.check(connObj.socket):
                                time.sleep(0.1)
                                continue

                            # send SIGINT to profiler #
                            sig = connObj.recv()
                            try:
                                os.kill(pid, signal.SIGINT)
                            except:
                                _printErr(
                                    "failed to send SIGINT to %s"
                                    % SysMgr.getTaskStr(pid),
                                    True,
                                )
            except ValueError:
                pass
            except SystemExit:
                sys.exit(0)
            except:
                _printErr("failed to watch events from channels", True)
                sys.exit(-1)

        # terminate #
        sys.exit(0)

    # block signal #
    SysMgr.blockSignal(SysMgr.commSig, "block")

    while 1:
        if not SysMgr.requestQueue:
            break

        # pop a command #
        try:
            req = SysMgr.requestQueue.pop(0)
            connObj, cmd, cliProc = req
        except SystemExit:
            sys.exit(0)
        except:
            SysMgr.printErr("failed to pop a request command", True)
            continue

        # handle a command #
        try:
            # reply ACK #
            connObj.send(b"ACK")

            # execute a command #
            _handleCmd(connObj, cmd, cliProc)
        except SystemExit:
            sys.exit(0)
        except:
            _printErr(
                "failed to handle '%s' command from %s" % (cmd, cliProc),
                True,
            )
            sys.exit(-1)

    # unblock signal #
    SysMgr.blockSignal(SysMgr.commSig, "unblock")

def reqHandler(signum, frame):
    # define error message sender function #
    def _sendErrMsg(connObj, msg):
        connObj.write('{"error": %s}' % msg)

    def _sendInfoMsg(connObj, msg):
        connObj.write('{"info": %s}' % msg)

    def _printErr(msg, reason=False):
        SysMgr.printErr(msg, reason=reason)
        _sendErrMsg(connObj, msg)

    def _printInfo(msg):
        SysMgr.printInfo(msg)
        _sendInfoMsg(connObj, msg)

    # define command handler #
    def _handleCmd(connObj, cmd, cliProc):
        # update event status #
        SysMgr.updateEventStatus()

        # set final command #
        cmdList = cmd.split("|")
        mainCmd = cmdList[0].lower()
        cmdOpt = cmdList[1:] if len(cmdList) > 1 else []

        # handle watch request #
        if mainCmd == "watch":
            SysMgr.printInfo("registered %s for event watch" % cliProc)
            SysMgr.eventSubList.append([cliProc, connObj])
            return
        # handle limit request #
        elif SysMgr.isLimitMode(mainCmd):
            # save target flags #
            origProcessEnable = SysMgr.processEnable
            origTargetExe = SysMgr.environList.pop("TARGETEXE", None)
            origTargetCmd = SysMgr.environList.pop("TARGETCMD", None)

            # set target flags #
            upperOpts = list(map(str.upper, cmdOpt))
            SysMgr.processEnable = "PROCESS" in upperOpts
            for name in ("TARGETEXE", "TARGETCMD"):
                if name in upperOpts:
                    SysMgr.environList[name] = ["SET"]

            # apply limit #
            try:
                ret = SysMgr.applyLimitVars(
                    {mainCmd.upper(): [":".join(cmdOpt[:1])]}
                )
                if ret:
                    _printInfo(
                        "applied %s command for %s"
                        % (
                            cmd,
                            SysMgr.getCommList(
                                ret,
                                cmdline="TARGETCMD" in SysMgr.environList,
                            ),
                        )
                    )
                else:
                    raise Exception("no task")
            except:
                _printErr("failed to execute %s command" % cmd)

            # close connection #
            try:
                connObj.close()
            except:
                pass
            finally:
                # recover process flag #
                SysMgr.processEnable = origProcessEnable
                for flag, name in (
                    (origTargetExe, "TARGETEXE"),
                    (origTargetCmd, "TARGETCMD"),
                ):
                    if not flag:
                        SysMgr.environList.pop(name, None)
                return

        # check the maximum number of jobs #
        nrMaxJob = UtilMgr.getEnvironNum("NRMAXJOB", False, 0, False)
        if nrMaxJob:
            nrJobs = len(SysMgr.getEventTaskList(json=True))
            if nrMaxJob <= nrJobs:
                _printErr(
                    (
                        "failed to run a new job "
                        "because %s jobs are already running (MAX:%s)"
                    )
                    % (UtilMgr.convNum(nrJobs), UtilMgr.convNum(nrMaxJob))
                )
                return

        # backup items before worker task creation #
        localServObj = SysMgr.localServObj
        eventCommandList = SysMgr.eventCommandList

        # check save mode #
        isSaveMode = mainCmd in ("save", "saveraw", "savemin")

        # create a new worker process and parent return #
        pid = SysMgr.createProcess()
        if pid > 0:
            # close connection to decrease refcnt of socket in parent #
            connObj.close()

            # register event task info #
            SysMgr.addEventInfo(cmd, pid)

            # increase report number #
            if isSaveMode or mainCmd in ("cpuprof", "memprof"):
                SysMgr.nrReport += 1

            return

        # apply new options #
        sys.argv = sys.argv[:2]
        optStr = " ".join(cmdOpt)
        if optStr:
            try:
                SysMgr.updateEnvironVars(optStr)
                SysMgr.parseAnalOption(optStr)
            except:
                _printErr("failed to apply options '%s'" % optStr, True)
                return

        # set default handling environment #
        SysMgr.setSignal(SysMgr.commSig, signal.SIG_IGN)
        SysMgr.setSignal(signal.SIGINT, SysMgr.termHandler)
        SysMgr.clearExitFunc()
        SysMgr.encodeEnable = False
        SysMgr.colorEnable = False
        SysMgr.printEnable = True
        SysMgr.streamEnable = True
        SysMgr.eventCommandList = eventCommandList
        SysMgr.progressEnable = False
        origOutPath = SysMgr.outPath
        if origOutPath:
            origOutPath = SysMgr.convFullPath(origOutPath)
        SysMgr.outPath = None
        if not SysMgr.getOption("i"):
            SysMgr.intervalEnable = 1

        # mark SERVERTASK #
        SysMgr.addEnvironVar("SERVERTASK")

        # define a socket monitor function #
        def _monitorConn(connObj):
            # wait for disconnection #
            while 1:
                try:
                    # wait for socket event #
                    selectObj = SysMgr.getPkg("select")
                    selectObj.select([connObj.socket], [], [])

                    # check socket event #
                    if NetworkMgr.check(connObj.socket):
                        time.sleep(0.1)
                        continue

                    SysMgr.printInfo(
                        "disconnected from %s for '%s' command"
                        % (cliProc, cmd)
                    )
                    os.kill(os.getpid(), signal.SIGINT)
                    return
                except SystemExit:
                    sys.exit(0)
                except:
                    SysMgr.printWarn(
                        "failed to monitor the socket for %s of %s"
                        % (cmd, cliProc),
                        True,
                        True,
                    )

        # create a socket monitor task for handling disconnection #
        threadObj = SysMgr.getPkg("threading")
        tobj = threadObj.Thread(target=_monitorConn, args=[connObj])
        tobj.daemon = True
        tobj.start()

        # define notify function #
        def _printExecutedCmd():
            SysMgr.printInfo(
                "executed '%s' command from %s" % (cmd, cliProc)
            )

        # check command #
        isDrawMode = mainCmd in SysMgr.getCmdList().get("visual", {})
        if not (
            mainCmd
            in (
                "bdtop",
                "bdmon",
                "control",
                "cpuprof",
                "event",
                "dir",
                "dirlist",
                "download",
                "dump",
                "getconf",
                "getpeak",
                "getpkg",
                "getpkginfo",
                "getpkglist",
                "getpkglistinfo",
                "jobs",
                "kill",
                "logcat",
                "logmon",
                "memmon",
                "memprof",
                "memtop",
                "ps",
                "pslist",
                "remove",
                "resmon",
                "ressum",
                "restop",
                "restopsum",
                "save",
                "saveraw",
                "savemin",
                "sysdump",
                "sysinfo",
                "syslist",
                "taskresmon",
                "tkill",
            )
            or isDrawMode
        ):
            errMsg = "no support '%s' command" % mainCmd
            _printErr(errMsg)
            sys.exit(-1)

        # print status #
        _printExecutedCmd()

        # set task name #
        SysMgr.setComm("guider_" + mainCmd)

        # clear monitoring buffer #
        if (
            mainCmd
            not in (
                "dump",
                "sysdump",
            )
            and not isSaveMode
        ):
            SysMgr.clearProcBuffer()

        # -------------------- execute command -------------------- #
        if mainCmd in ("dump", "sysdump"):
            # summarize monitoring buffer #
            SysMgr.printFd = MemoryFile(name="buf")
            TaskAnalyzer.printIntervalUsage(
                onlySummary=True, onlyTotal=(mainCmd == "sysdump")
            )

            # send summary stats in JSON #
            SysMgr.jsonEnable = True
            SysMgr.printFd.seek(0)
            stats, memstat = TaskAnalyzer.getStatsFile(
                "tmp", handle=SysMgr.printFd
            )
            SysMgr.printFd = connObj
            connObj.write(UtilMgr.convDict2Str(stats, pretty=False))

        elif isSaveMode:
            # update output path #
            SysMgr.outPath = origOutPath

            # build command string #
            cmdstr = mainCmd.upper()
            if cmdOpt:
                cmdstr += ":" + "|".join(cmdOpt)

            # WARN: not support timer in this context #

            # define file path sender #
            def _pathSender(connObj, cmd):
                # get real path #
                path = SysMgr.lastReportPath
                if path:
                    name, ext = path.rsplit(".", 1)
                    name += "_%s" % SysMgr.pid
                    path = name + "." + ext

                # send result #
                pathDict = {
                    "cmd": cmd,
                    "path": path,
                    "size": UtilMgr.getFileSize(path, False),
                }
                connObj.write(UtilMgr.convDict2Str(pathDict, pretty=False))

            # register return function #
            SysMgr.addExitFunc(_pathSender, [connObj, cmd])

            # handle save command #
            try:
                SysMgr.topInstance.handleEventCmd(
                    cmdstr, "CUSTOM", False, False
                )
            except SystemExit:
                pass
            except:
                _printErr("failed to handle %s" % mainCmd, True)

        elif mainCmd in ("resmon", "taskresmon", "restop"):
            # start sending stats in JSON #
            SysMgr.jsonEnable = mainCmd in ("resmon", "taskresmon")
            SysMgr.printFd = connObj

            # add environ var only for task monitoring #
            if mainCmd == "taskresmon":
                SysMgr.addEnvironVar("TASKSTREAM")
                SysMgr.taskStreamEnable = True

            # return and continue #
            if SysMgr.isTopMode():
                return
            else:
                main("top")
                sys.exit(0)

        elif mainCmd in ("ressum", "restopsum"):
            if not cmdOpt:
                _printErr("no target file path")
                sys.exit(-1)
            for f in cmdOpt:
                if not os.path.exists(f):
                    _printErr("no '%s' file" % f)
                    sys.exit(-1)

            # start sending stats in JSON #
            SysMgr.jsonEnable = mainCmd in ("ressum")
            SysMgr.printFd = connObj

            # summarize report file #
            TaskAnalyzer.doSumReport(cmdOpt)

        elif mainCmd in ("getconf"):
            # send stats in JSON #
            SysMgr.jsonEnable = True

            # get config #
            confData = SysMgr.getConfigItem("threshold")

            connObj.write(UtilMgr.convDict2Str(confData, pretty=False))

        elif mainCmd in ("getpeak"):
            # send stats in JSON #
            SysMgr.jsonEnable = True

            # get peak items #
            peakList = SysMgr.getPeakList()

            connObj.write(UtilMgr.convDict2Str(peakList, pretty=False))

        elif mainCmd in ("bdmon", "bdtop"):
            # start sending stats in JSON #
            SysMgr.jsonEnable = mainCmd == "bdmon"
            SysMgr.printFd = connObj

            # start monitoring binder calls #
            SysMgr.addEnvironVar("FLUSH")
            SysMgr.addEnvironVar("CONSUME")
            targets = [
                "binder/binder_transaction",
                "binder/binder_transaction_received",
            ]
            cb = LogMgr.binderHandler
            parser = LogMgr.parseBinderData
            LogMgr.printTrace(False, targets=targets, cb=cb, parser=parser)

        elif mainCmd in ("remove", "download"):
            if not cmdOpt:
                _printErr("no target file path")
                sys.exit(-1)

            # define getter #
            def _getPacket(fd):
                while 1:
                    try:
                        packet = fd.read(connObj.sendSize)
                        if packet == b"":
                            raise Exception()
                        yield packet
                    except GeneratorExit:
                        return
                    except SystemExit:
                        sys.exit(0)
                    except:
                        yield None

            def _remove(d):
                flist = UtilMgr.getFileList(
                    [d], sort=True, exceptDir=False
                )
                res = SysMgr.removeFiles(flist)
                if res:
                    _printInfo("removed '%s'" % ",".join(res))
                else:
                    _printErr("no removed file")

            def _download(d):
                try:
                    fd = open(d, "rb")
                except SystemExit:
                    sys.exit(0)
                except:
                    _printErr("failed to open '%s'" % d, True)
                    sys.exit(-1)

                # start sending file data #
                for packet in _getPacket(fd):
                    if not packet:
                        break
                    connObj.write(packet)

            # handle files #
            for d in cmdOpt:
                # validate path #
                if "NOLIMITPATH" in SysMgr.environList:
                    pass
                # no out path #
                elif not origOutPath:
                    _printErr("no output path in Guider")
                    sys.exit(-1)
                # absolute path #
                elif d.startswith("/"):
                    if not d.startswith(origOutPath):
                        _printErr("'%s' is not in '%s'" % (d, origOutPath))
                        sys.exit(-1)
                # relative path #
                elif not os.path.exists(os.path.join(origOutPath, d)):
                    _printErr("'%s' is not in '%s'" % (d, origOutPath))
                    sys.exit(-1)

                # remove #
                if mainCmd == "remove":
                    _remove(d)
                # download #
                elif mainCmd == "download":
                    _download(d)

        elif mainCmd in ("dir", "dirlist"):
            if not cmdOpt:
                if not origOutPath:
                    _printErr("no target directory")
                    sys.exit(-1)
                if not os.path.exists(origOutPath):
                    cmdOpt = [os.path.dirname(origOutPath)]
                elif os.path.isdir(origOutPath):
                    cmdOpt = [origOutPath]
                else:
                    cmdOpt = [os.path.dirname(origOutPath)]

            # start sending stats in JSON #
            SysMgr.jsonEnable = mainCmd == "dirlist"
            SysMgr.printFd = connObj
            SysMgr.showAll = True

            # check dir #
            cmdOpt = UtilMgr.cleanItem(cmdOpt)
            if not cmdOpt:
                _printErr("failed to get directory info")
                sys.exit(-1)

            # list directories #
            for d in cmdOpt:
                SysMgr.printDirs(d)

        elif mainCmd in (
            "getpkg",
            "getpkginfo",
            "getpkglist",
            "getpkglistinfo",
        ):
            # start sending stats in JSON #
            SysMgr.jsonEnable = mainCmd in ("getpkglist", "getpkglistinfo")
            SysMgr.printFd = connObj
            SysMgr.showAll = True

            # get package list #
            if "info" in mainCmd:
                SysMgr.addEnvironVar("GETPKGLISTINFO")
            else:
                SysMgr.addEnvironVar("GETPKGLIST")
            SysMgr.doPerfetto()

        elif mainCmd in ("sysinfo", "syslist"):
            # start sending stats in JSON #
            SysMgr.jsonEnable = mainCmd == "syslist"
            SysMgr.printFd = connObj

            # print system info #
            SysMgr.doPrintInfo()

        elif mainCmd in ("ps", "pslist"):
            # start sending stats in JSON #
            SysMgr.jsonEnable = mainCmd == "pslist"
            SysMgr.printFd = connObj

            # print process list #
            SysMgr.doPs()

        elif mainCmd in ("memtop", "memmon"):
            if not cmdOpt:
                _printErr("no target info")
                sys.exit(-1)

            # start sending stats in JSON #
            SysMgr.jsonEnable = mainCmd == "memmon"
            SysMgr.printFd = connObj

            # start monitoring memory #
            SysMgr.addEnvironVar("MEMTOP")
            SysMgr.doPerfetto()

        elif mainCmd in ("logcat", "logmon"):
            # start sending stats in JSON #
            SysMgr.jsonEnable = mainCmd == "logmon"
            SysMgr.printFd = connObj

            # start printing android logs #
            LogMgr.printAndLog(True)

        elif mainCmd == "jobs":
            # set JSON attribute #
            SysMgr.jsonEnable = True

            # send event task list #
            connObj.write(
                UtilMgr.convDict2Str(
                    SysMgr.getEventTaskList(), pretty=False
                )
            )

        elif mainCmd in ("kill", "tkill"):
            # set JSON attribute #
            SysMgr.jsonEnable = True

            isThread = mainCmd == "tkill"

            # convert target list #
            try:
                argList = UtilMgr.cleanItem(("".join(cmdOpt)).split(","))
            except SystemExit:
                sys.exit(0)
            except:
                pass

            # convert items to pid #
            targets = SysMgr.convTaskList(argList, isThread=isThread)
            if targets:
                targetList = targets
            else:
                targetList = argList

            # send signal #
            ret = SysMgr.sendSignalProcs(
                signal.SIGKILL, targetList, isThread=isThread
            )

            # send event task list #
            connObj.write(UtilMgr.convDict2Str(ret, pretty=False))

        elif mainCmd in ("control", "event"):
            if not cmdOpt:
                _printErr("no %s value" % mainCmd)
                sys.exit(-1)

            # create a UDP socket #
            cliObj = NetworkMgr(
                "client", localServObj.ip, localServObj.port, tcp=False
            )

            # send commands #
            for ccmd in cmdOpt:
                # make command #
                ccmd = ccmd.upper()
                if ccmd.startswith("EVENT_CMD_"):
                    pass
                elif ccmd.startswith("CMD_"):
                    ccmd = "EVENT_" + ccmd
                else:
                    ccmd = "EVENT_CMD_" + ccmd

                if mainCmd == "event":
                    ccmd = ccmd.replace("_CMD", "")

                # send command #
                cliObj.send(ccmd)

                _printInfo(
                    "sent '%s' command to server(%s:%s)"
                    % (ccmd, cliObj.ip, cliObj.port)
                )

        elif mainCmd in ("cpuprof", "memprof") or isDrawMode:
            # get select object #
            selectObj = SysMgr.getPkg("select")

            # add environment var to merge env list #
            SysMgr.addEnvironVar("MERGEENVLIST")

            # set command set #
            if isDrawMode:
                gcmd = mainCmd
            elif mainCmd == "cpuprof":
                gcmd = "sperf"
            elif mainCmd == "memprof":
                gcmd = "hprof"
            else:
                _printErr(
                    "failed to recognize '%s' command from %s"
                    % (mainCmd, cliProc)
                )
                sys.exit(-1)

            cmdSet = [gcmd] + cmdOpt + ["-J"]

            # cleanup directories #
            SysMgr.cleanupDirs()

            # set report path #
            SysMgr.outPath = origOutPath
            SysMgr.setReportPath(origOutPath, mainCmd)

            # launch Guider command #
            try:
                ret = SysMgr.launchGuider(
                    cmdSet,
                    pipe=True,
                    stderr=True,
                    stream=True,
                    logo=False,
                    log=False,
                    keepOut=True,
                )
                if not ret:
                    sys.exit(-1)
            except SystemExit:
                sys.exit(0)
            except:
                _printErr(
                    "failed to launch %s(%s)"
                    % (__module__, " ".join(cmdSet)),
                    reason=True,
                )
                sys.exit(-1)

            # get pid and pipe #
            pid, pipe = ret
            pipeObj = os.fdopen(pipe.fileno(), "r")
            SysMgr.setBlock(pipe.fileno(), False)

            # remove socket timeout #
            connObj.timeout(None)

            try:
                while 1:
                    [rdList, _, _] = selectObj.select(
                        [pipeObj, connObj.socket], [], [], 1
                    )
                    for rd in rdList:
                        # final output from launcher #
                        if rd == pipeObj:
                            res = pipeObj.read()
                            if res:
                                connObj.write(res)
                            connObj.close()
                            raise ValueError()
                        # signal from client #
                        elif rd == connObj.socket:
                            # check socket event #
                            if not NetworkMgr.check(connObj.socket):
                                time.sleep(0.1)
                                continue

                            # send SIGINT to profiler #
                            sig = connObj.recv()
                            try:
                                os.kill(pid, signal.SIGINT)
                            except:
                                _printErr(
                                    "failed to send SIGINT to %s"
                                    % SysMgr.getTaskStr(pid),
                                    True,
                                )
            except ValueError:
                pass
            except SystemExit:
                sys.exit(0)
            except:
                _printErr("failed to watch events from channels", True)
                sys.exit(-1)

        # terminate #
        sys.exit(0)

    # block signal #
    SysMgr.blockSignal(SysMgr.commSig, "block")

    while 1:
        if not SysMgr.requestQueue:
            break

        # pop a command #
        try:
            req = SysMgr.requestQueue.pop(0)
            connObj, cmd, cliProc = req
        except SystemExit:
            sys.exit(0)
        except:
            SysMgr.printErr("failed to pop a request command", True)
            continue

        # handle a command #
        try:
            # reply ACK #
            connObj.send(b"ACK")

            # execute a command #
            _handleCmd(connObj, cmd, cliProc)
        except SystemExit:
            sys.exit(0)
        except:
            _printErr(
                "failed to handle '%s' command from %s" % (cmd, cliProc),
                True,
            )
            sys.exit(-1)

    # unblock signal #
    SysMgr.blockSignal(SysMgr.commSig, "unblock")

def checkEnv():
    def _checkMode():
        # check args #
        if len(sys.argv) == 1:
            return True

        # get command #
        cmd = sys.argv[1]

        # common commands for all OS #
        if cmd in (
            "addr2sym",
            "attop",
            "bdtop",
            "bgtop",
            "cli",
            "comp",
            "convert",
            "cputest",
            "ctop",
            "decomp",
            "dirdiff",
            "disktop",
            "drawreq",
            "elftree",
            "exec",
            "fserver",
            "helptest",
            "hserver",
            "iotest",
            "less",
            "list",
            "merge",
            "mkcache",
            "ntop",
            "ping",
            "print",
            "printdir",
            "printdlt",
            "printext",
            "pstree",
            "readelf",
            "report",
            "req",
            "rtop",
            "send",
            "split",
            "strings",
            "sym2addr",
            "tail",
            "top",
            "topdiff",
            "topsum",
            "tptop",
            "trtop",
        ):
            return True
        # only commands for MacOS #
        elif SysMgr.isDarwin and cmd in (
            "dlttop",
            "ftop",
            "kill",
            "printdlt",
            "server",
        ):
            return True
        # specific commands for all OS #
        elif SysMgr.isDrawMode() or SysMgr.isHelpMode():
            return True

        return False

    # save platform name #
    SysMgr.platform = sys.platform

    def _checkOS():
        if len(sys.argv) == 1:
            pass
        elif not _checkMode():
            SysMgr.colorEnable = False
            if sys.argv[1] in SysMgr.getCmdSet():
                SysMgr.printErr(
                    "'%s' is not supported on '%s' platform"
                    % (sys.argv[1], SysMgr.platform)
                )
            else:
                SysMgr.printErr("no command '%s'" % sys.argv[1])
            sys.exit(-1)

    # Linux #
    if SysMgr.platform.startswith("linux"):
        SysMgr.isLinux = True

        # environment variables #
        try:
            # set default terminal to xterm #
            os.environ["TERM"] = "xterm"

            # set run type #
            if "REMOTERUN" in os.environ:
                SysMgr.encodeEnable = False
                SysMgr.remoteRun = True
                SysMgr.colorEnable = False
            else:
                SysMgr.colorEnable = True

            # check encode condition #
            if not "LANG" in os.environ or "tty" in os.ttyname(
                sys.stdout.fileno()
            ):
                SysMgr.encodeEnable = False
        except SystemExit:
            sys.exit(0)
        except:
            pass

        # set force color flag #
        SysMgr.forceColorEnable = SysMgr.isTraceMode()

        # android #
        if "ANDROID_ROOT" in os.environ and not "NO_ANDROID" in os.environ:
            SysMgr.isAndroid = True
            SysMgr.libcPath = "libc"
            SysMgr.libcppPath = "libstdc++"
            if os.path.exists("/system/lib/libgccdemangle.so"):
                SysMgr.libdemanglePath = "libgccdemangle"
            else:
                SysMgr.libdemanglePath = "libbacktrace"
            SysMgr.cacheDirPath = "/data/log/guider"

    # Windows #
    elif SysMgr.platform.startswith("win"):
        SysMgr.isLinux = False
        SysMgr.isWindows = True

        _checkOS()

        # set default SIGINT handler #
        SysMgr.setSignal(signal.SIGINT, SysMgr.stopHandler)

        # set color flag #
        SysMgr.colorEnable = False

    # MacOS #
    elif SysMgr.platform.startswith("darwin"):
        SysMgr.isLinux = False
        SysMgr.isDarwin = True
        ConfigMgr.SIG_LIST = ConfigMgr.SIG_LIST_MACOS

        _checkOS()

        # set color flag #
        SysMgr.colorEnable = True
    else:
        SysMgr.printErr("'%s' platform is not supported" % SysMgr.platform)
        sys.exit(-1)

    # check locale #
    try:
        if not SysMgr.encodeEnable:
            pass
        elif "NO_ENCODE" in os.environ:
            SysMgr.encodeEnable = False
        else:
            lang = os.getenv("LANG")
            if not lang or not "UTF" in lang:
                SysMgr.encodeEnable = False
    except SystemExit:
        sys.exit(0)
    except:
        pass

    # check python #
    if sys.version_info < (2, 6):
        SysMgr.printWarn(
            "some features may not work on python %d.%d"
            % (sys.version_info[0], sys.version_info[1]),
            True,
        )

def test_premature_loading(self):
    """Test premature policy loading

    Verifies that loading policies by way of admin context before
    populating extensions and extending the resource map results in
    networks with router:external is true being invisible to regular
    tenants.
    """
    extension_manager = extensions.ExtensionManager(self.extension_path)
    admin_context = context.get_admin_context()
    tenant_context = context.Context('test_user', 'test_project_id', False)
    extension_manager.extend_resources(self.api_version,
                                       attributes.RESOURCES)
    self.assertTrue(self._check_external_router_policy(admin_context))
    self.assertFalse(self._check_external_router_policy(tenant_context))

def split_into_lines(
    string: str, width_tuple: tuple[int, int, int]
) -> list[str]:
    """Splits string into a list of substrings at whitespace.

    `width_tuple` is a 3-tuple of `(first_width, last_width, middle_width)`.
    The first substring has a length not longer than `first_width`, the last
    substring has a length not longer than `last_width`, and all other
    substrings have a length not longer than `middle_width`.
    `string` may contain ANSI codes at word borders.
    """
    first_width, middle_width, last_width = width_tuple
    words = []

    if uncolorize(string) == string:
        # No colors in string
        words = string.split()
    else:
        # Use a regex to find escapes and the text within them.
        for m in ESC_TEXT_REGEX.finditer(string):
            # m contains four groups:
            # pretext - any text before escape sequence
            # esc - intitial escape sequence
            # text - text, no escape sequence, may contain spaces
            # reset - ASCII colour reset
            space_before_text = False
            if m.group("pretext") != "":
                # Some pretext found, let's handle it
                # Add any words in the pretext
                words += m.group("pretext").split()
                if m.group("pretext")[-1] == " ":
                    # Pretext ended on a space
                    space_before_text = True
                else:
                    # Pretext ended mid-word, ensure next word
                    pass
            else:
                # pretext empty, treat as if there is a space before
                space_before_text = True
            if m.group("text")[0] == " ":
                # First character of the text is a space
                space_before_text = True
            # Now, handle the words in the main text:
            raw_words = m.group("text").split()
            if space_before_text:
                # Colorize each word with pre/post escapes
                # Reconstruct colored words
                words += [
                    f"{m['esc']}{raw_word}{RESET_COLOR}"
                    for raw_word in raw_words
                ]
            elif raw_words:
                # Pretext stops mid-word
                if m.group("esc") != RESET_COLOR:
                    # Add the rest of the current word, with a reset after it
                    words[-1] += f"{m['esc']}{raw_words[0]}{RESET_COLOR}"
                    # Add the subsequent colored words:
                    words += [
                        f"{m['esc']}{raw_word}{RESET_COLOR}"
                        for raw_word in raw_words[1:]
                    ]
                else:
                    # Caught a mid-word escape sequence
                    words[-1] += raw_words[0]
                    words += raw_words[1:]
            if (
                m.group("text")[-1] != " "
                and m.group("posttext") != ""
                and m.group("posttext")[0] != " "
            ):
                # reset falls mid-word
                post_text = m.group("posttext").split()
                words[-1] += post_text[0]
                words += post_text[1:]
            else:
                # Add any words after escape sequence
                words += m.group("posttext").split()
    result: list[str] = []
    next_substr = ""
    # Iterate over all words.
    previous_fit = False
    for i in range(len(words)):
        if i == 0:
            pot_substr = words[i]
        else:
            # (optimistically) add the next word to check the fit
            pot_substr = " ".join([next_substr, words[i]])
        # Find out if the pot(ential)_substr fits into the next substring.
        fits_first = len(result) == 0 and color_len(pot_substr) <= first_width
        fits_middle = len(result) != 0 and color_len(pot_substr) <= middle_width
        if fits_first or fits_middle:
            # Fitted(!) let's try and add another word before appending
            next_substr = pot_substr
            previous_fit = True
        elif not fits_first and not fits_middle and previous_fit:
            # Extra word didn't fit, append what we have
            result.append(next_substr)
            next_substr = words[i]
            previous_fit = color_len(next_substr) <= middle_width
        else:
            # Didn't fit anywhere
            if uncolorize(pot_substr) == pot_substr:
                # Simple uncolored string, append a cropped word
                if len(result) == 0:
                    # Crop word by the first_width for the first line
                    result.append(pot_substr[:first_width])
                    # add rest of word to next line
                    next_substr = pot_substr[first_width:]
                else:
                    result.append(pot_substr[:middle_width])
                    next_substr = pot_substr[middle_width:]
            else:
                # Colored strings
                if len(result) == 0:
                    this_line, next_line = color_split(pot_substr, first_width)
                    result.append(this_line)
                    next_substr = next_line
                else:
                    this_line, next_line = color_split(pot_substr, middle_width)
                    result.append(this_line)
                    next_substr = next_line
            previous_fit = color_len(next_substr) <= middle_width

    # We finished constructing the substrings, but the last substring
    # has not yet been added to the result.
    result.append(next_substr)
    # Also, the length of the last substring was only checked against
    # `middle_width`. Append an empty substring as the new last substring if
    # the last substring is too long.
    if not color_len(next_substr) <= last_width:
        result.append("")
    return result
